title: Doctor JSON uses the explicit --cwd target
description: Black-box smoke for `armature doctor --json --cwd <dir>` with an isolated runtime home and a temporary target project.
input: Create a temporary target project with a package.json, run `armature doctor --json --cwd <dir>`, and verify the JSON output reports that target project's name and cwd.
command:
bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d); TMP_PROJECT=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP_HOME $TMP_PROJECT" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP_HOME/.armature"; cat > "$TMP_PROJECT/package.json" <<EOF
{"name":"doctor-json-cwd-box","devDependencies":{"vitest":"^1.0.0"}}
EOF
env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_HOME" ARMATURE_HOME="$TMP_HOME/.armature" ARMATURE_PROVIDER=openai OPENAI_API_KEY=test-openai-key node dist/bin/armature.js doctor --json --cwd "$TMP_PROJECT"'
